title: The One With A Chick. And A Duck

lines.text: Outside Central Perk, Rachel and Phoebe are helping Monica learn how to roller skate by rolling her between themselves.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So who's idea was it to put everybody in the diner on skates?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, some idiot customer put a suggestion in the suggestion box.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, they took my idea!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was you?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Okay, here you go. (rolls her back to Rachel)

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (bringing Rachel a mug) Rachel, I made you a cocoa.
[He distracts her from catching Monica and Monica slams into her, knocking her down. Monica then falls on top of her.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, are you guys okay?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Are you all right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (leaving Central Perk and seeing Monica laying on top of Rachel who is moaning in pain.) Oh my.
They both turn and give him a dirty look.
Opening Credits
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is watching Quincy M.E. as Chandler is walking into the living room from his bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sporting a goatee) Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. Y'know with that goatee you kinda look like Satan.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, so that's why the priest threw holy water on me. (there's no reaction from Joey) Okay, listen, you have to cheer up! Okay? You should come out with Ross and me, I mean anything is better than sitting around here crying all day about Kate.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey I was crying because, because nobody believed Quincy's theory. Okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (triumphantly) I'm gonna be on TV!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! They're putting together this panel to talk about these fossils they just found in Peru and The Discovery Channel's gonna film it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God! Who's gonna watch that?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. You ready to go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. (they start to leave)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Saw a girl with that vest.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks. (He takes off the vest and throws it on the floor.)

lines.speaker: Anchorwoman
lines.text: (on TV) While most of us think of chocolate bunnies and baskets as traditional Easter gifts. Some people insist on giving live chicks as presents. (Joey is intrigued by the idea) Unfortunately, the sad fact remains that most of these little guys won't live to see the fourth of July. (Joey starts to call a place to buy a little baby chick) Because of as a result of improper care, they will be dead.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on phone) Yeah, hi. You guys got any of those baby chicks? 'Cause I was watching this ah, commercial on TV and man, those guys are cute!
The Moondance Diner, Pete is entering, Monica is on roller skates.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi! Hey, Pete you're back! Hey, check this out. (She starts to skate over to him)

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Wow! Skates!
She gets just about all the way over to him and falls into his arms.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! You're a lot sturdier that Chandler. He crumpled like a piece of paper. So how was you're trip?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well... (he holds up a gift he brought her)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, what'd ya bring me?! (She opens the gift) Awww, hotel toiletries from Japan. Oh, these are gonna go in my permanent collection. You want some coffee?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Yeah, sure, that'd be great.
She starts to go and get the coffee and falls behind the counter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (popping back up) Regular or decaf?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Ah, which ever is closest.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (hands him a cup)

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: So ask me what I did today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So what did you do today Pete?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I bought a restaurant and I would like you to be the head chef.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Oh.(She turns around quickly and falls)
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is telling Rachel about Pete's offer.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can you believe he just offered me a restaurant?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What a jerk! You want me to kick his ass?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean this has been like my dream since I got my first Easy Bake Oven and opened Easy Monica's Bakery. I mean I would kill for this job. I mean I can totally do this job, and God knows I paid my dues. (She removes her fake breasts) But Pete's just doing this because he has a crush on me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And you're still not attracted to him at all?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hmm, no. I mean how can I accept a restaurant from him? I-I-I-I can't. I couldn't even accept a necklace from Stu Vincent in the seventh grade.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but Mon that's totally different. He was you're health teacher.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, please.
She slaps Rachel's side and Rachel screams in pain.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? Honey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I am, my side still hurts from when you crashed into me yesterday.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God, I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.
Monica hugs her goes and hugs her

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering, wearing a white suit with a little red bow tie) Hey, you guys! Guess what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking at the outfit) Got a job on a river boat?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what I didn't wear this suit for a year because you hated it. Well, guess what? You're not my girlfriend anymore so...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh I see, so this suit is making a point.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now that you're on you're own, you're free to look as stupid as you like.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Monica) You like it right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh absolutely. I like it even more on you than I did on Colonel Sanders. (Ross starts to leave) Ross! Ross! I'm kidding!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, come here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What-what was it you were gonna tell us?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Oh! Was how you invented the cotton gin?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, good bye! (leaves)
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is talking to Phoebe about her suggestion.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So um, after you put the suggestion in the box, how long did it take for the roller skating thing to happen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, oh, about three months.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so I guess that's about ah, two weeks before the topless thing kicks in.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering carrying a box) Hey!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I got you something! Open it! Open it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (He opens it and it's a baby chick) It's a chicken.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's cute, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa, you guys, do you know anything about chicks?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fowl? No. Women? Nooo.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well they are a huge responsibility, especially at this age. They require constant care. They-they need just the right food, and lot's and lot's of love.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, well no problem there. (He picks up the chick, hugs it really tight, and talks to it like it's a little baby.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Easy Lenny.
The Moondance Diner, Pete is talking to Monica about the restaurant.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: So? I mean have you thought about it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Here's the thing.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Oh no, not the thing. I hate the thing. What's the thing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't do it. I'm sorry, I wish I could, but umm, see you have these feelings for me....

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait, that's-that's what you're worried about? If that's the problem, we've got no problem.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: No! Look, I was gonna tell you this over dinner, but I met somebody else. On my trip.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Her name's Ann, she's a journalist. Ahh, we met on the plane. She asked me if she could finish off my peanuts, I thought she said something else, we had a big laugh. Yeah, I just, I mean I got, I got tired of waiting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that's great! I mean I'm-I'm sorry, but I'm so happy for you. And now I can work for you!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I guess you can.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh, this is incredible! Ohh! (she rolls over and kisses him on the cheek) All right, y'know what? I'm just gonna roll right into that office and-and quit!

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (she gets ready to go) Can you give me a little push?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Yeah, sure. Good luck!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (rolling towards the office) I'm quitting!! Woo-hoo! (She rolls through a doorway and out of sight. We then hear a big crash, and see Monica roll past the door the other way.) I'm okay!! I'm all right!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Pete) Wow! That's exciting, you went to Japan, made up a woman.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm just saying, this woman, I mean she's fictitious. No?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Why would you say that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 'Cause you're still into Monica. So you told her there was somebody else so she would agree to work with you, so 'cause you figure oh if you spent a lot of time together, maybe something might happen, and...

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: You're good. You're good!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, no, I'm fairly intuitive and psychic. It's a substantial gift.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Listen, can you promise me that you won't tell her though?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Absolutely, oh I promise. Tell her what?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Thanks a lot.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I'm serious. I mean I'm intuitive, but my memory sucks.
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is baby-chick sitting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, but this is the last time. (singing) With a chick-chick here, and a chick-chick there. Here a chick, there a chick, everywhere a chick-chick-(Joey enters)-chickeeeen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How's she doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well yeah, don't-don't you think it's a she?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know. (He picks the chick up and turns it over, trying to determine the sex of the chick, and blows on it.) I can't tell, what ever it was went back in too quickly.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, anyway, I got to go change, I'm ah, meeting some of the cast for drinks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I stayed home from work today while you were at rehearsal so somebody could be here with our chick!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Who was up from 2 o'clock this morning until 5 o'clock this morning trying to get her back to sleep?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't think I get up when you get up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohhh, here it comes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, here it comes! I'm stuck here all day, and then you come in and spend two seconds with us and then expect to go off gallivanting with your friends? Well I don't think so mister!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!! I need to relax! Okay? I was working all day!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And you don't think taking care of our chick is work?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's not what I said. Okay, I just meant...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know what you meant!! (pause) You notice that ever since we got this chick, we've been fighting a lot more than we used too?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, maybe we weren't ready to have a chick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll take her back tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you think we'll get our three bucks back?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering carrying a garment bag) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sees the chick) Ohhh, hey! All right, listen, I-I have that TV thing in like two hours, and I need your help, okay? What do you think? (takes out two suits) This blue suit, or this brown one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, the brown one brings out your eyes, but your butt looks great in the blue one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really? (Joey gives him a 'Like I would know' look)
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is trying to take some aspirin.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she reaches for the bottle) Oww! (She grabs the bottle, but has trouble opening it. She pops the top off and aspirins fly all over the place as Ross enters.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! That aspirin dance really works!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (She bends over to try and pick up the aspirin) Oww!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, is that still...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm fine, I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No you're not.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes I am!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, I'm fine. Watch. (She picks up an aspirin between her toes) Look at that. (She lifts her leg to grab the aspirin with her hand and almost falls over.) Whoa-whoa!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stopping her from falling) Okay, okay. Look, you have got to go to a doctor! Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. I have got to get ready and go to a dinner at my bosses house. It's a very big deal, there's a lot of people there I have to meet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And I'm sure you're gonna make a big impression. Hi! I'm Rachel Green. It's nice to meet you. (He lifts his leg and imitates shaking hands with it, just like how Rachel was trying to pick up the aspirin with her feet.) Come on, you probably have a broken rib!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I will go to the hospital tomorrow, it'll still be broken then.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But y'know, I could use a hand getting ready.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, either help me or go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine. I'll go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (with a hurt expression on her face) Okay, but before you go, could you help me first?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (He checks his watch) Sure. I'll help you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (rushing in) Oh, good! Good! Do you guys know how to get a chick out of a VCR?!
Commercial Break
Rachel's bedroom, Rachel is trying to put on eye liner with her left hand, as Ross is setting out her shoes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (She drops the brush) Y'know what? I cannot do this with my left hand! Would you please, help me with this too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh. (He drops the shoes, takes the brush from her, and licks the tip. He doesn't like how it tastes.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (taking the brush back) Okay. Let's use this brush. (Hands him another one.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. This stuff?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Careful. Light. Okay, do you know how, just sweep it across the lid. Okay? Just sweep it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oke-dokey. (He pokes her in the eye with the brush.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-ho!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! That's just poking me in the eye!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry, I'm sorry. Close, close, close...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, just sweep it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sweeping...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweep, sweep....(He starts to paint it on her eye, making it look like she has a black eye.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, now make it even, 'cause we don't...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We don't want it-it to be too much, we want it to be subtle.
Of course it's too late for that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. No, y'know you don't, you don't wear enough of this. (Rachel is shocked) What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Since when, since when do you think I don't wear enough of this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I, close your eyes, I just think you're gonna like this a little better, 'cause, close-close... (He gets some more on the brush)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Blow it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (blows it) Sorry. 'Cause umm, I think this will make you a little more sophisticated.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sophisticated like a hooker?
Central Perk, Monica is entering, Phoebe is already there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, guess what I'm doing tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm checking out the restaurant with Pete.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, Monica, I am so excited for you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, I have to tell you something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But I can't tell you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, but wouldn't it be easier if you had to tell me something that you could tell me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, sure in a perfect world. But, no, I promised I wouldn't tell, and I swore to like all my gods.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Does it have to do with Ross and Rachel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does it have to do with Joey?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does it have to do with-with Chandler and that sock that he keeps by his bed?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, but let's come back to that later!
Rachel's Bedroom, Ross is finishing up her make-up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There you go! Good enough for your party, huh?
She turns and looks in the mirror, and it's way, way over done. She looks like she has two black eyes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure, I'll just sit next to the trans-sexual from purchasing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, come on! All right, I gotta go! So good luck at the party. Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wait, Ross, would you just stay and help me get dressed?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (checks his watch) Sure, okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Okay, great! Umm, okay, just turn around.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't want you to see me naked!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel, I've seen you naked a million times. I ate hot fudge off you naked. Remember, I-I sucked that mini-marshmallow out of your belly button?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but that was different. Y'know? I mean, we were, we were going out then, now I think it's weird.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, y'know I can see you naked any time I want.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All I have to do is close my eyes. See? (closes his eyes) Woo-hoo!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross! Stop that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on! I don't want you thinking of me like that any more!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahh, sorry, nothing you can do about it. It's one of my ah, rights as the ex-boyfriend. (closes his eyes again) Oop, oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Stop it! Cut it out! Cut it out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, I'm sorry, it will never happen... (closes eyes) Uh-oh! Wait a minute! Wait-wait, now there are a hundred of you and I'm the king.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Rosss...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, would you grow up? It's no big deal.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. (She starts to take off her robe) Fine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yowzah!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: O-kay!! See what you did, I'm gonna be doing it by myself now. Okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aww, come on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's it. (She crawls onto the bed) Ow!!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-ow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow! Ow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Easy. Easy. You have to go to the hospital. Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I really do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I'm gonna get your coat and then I'll-I'll put you in a cab.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Oh wait, wait-wait, you're not gonna come with me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (He thinks about it) Of course I am. I just have to make a call.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay? (goes into the living room)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. (She goes to take off her make-up and screams in pain) Oww!!!! God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (rushing back in) What?! I wh-, what's wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, I just can't go to the hospital lookin' like this.
Central Perk, Monica is trying to find out what Phoebe won't tell her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does it involve travel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Noo!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does it involve clogs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, wait, wait. Clogs, or claws?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Clogs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Claws?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, so it doesn't involve Ross or Rachel or Chandler or Joey. But, what about Pete?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Shaking her head yes) No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is it?! What about Pete?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know! (frantically points at Monica)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I feel like I'm talking to Lassie. All right, Phoebe would you just tell me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I gotta go. (gets up)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I, but you're so close! No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, does it involve something to do with Pete's computer company?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, just go. You're never gonna get it!
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is watching Baywatch with the baby chick. He's on one leather chair, the chick is on the other. It's watching Yasmine Bleeth run and is chirping.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know. See, yes. That's Yasmine Bleeth, she's a completely different kind of chick. I love you both. But in very different ways.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sees he's watching Baywatch) Ohhh. (sees he still has the chick) Ahh! What are you doing?! I thought you were gonna take her back to the store today.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I did! But the store wouldn't take her back! So then I took her to the shelter, and you know what I found out?
Joey wants to know, but Chandler doesn't want to discuss it by the chick, so he and Joey move over to the windows and away from the chick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If they can't find a home for her, they kill her! And I'm not gonna let that happen to little Yasmine!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, good, good, good, 'cause, good, 'cause I was kinda having second thoughts too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. And it's not just chicks y'know? It's all kinds of other animals!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's horrible! Well, you did the right thing man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks, I'm glad you see it that way.
He hear a duck start quacking, and see it waddle into the living room from the bathroom. Joey wants to know what's with the duck.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohhh-hoo, funny story!
Pete's Restaurant's Kitchen, Pete is showing Monica around the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't believe this! Wow, look at this refrigerator! It's gigantic! I mean I could live in this thing! I'd be cold, but I'm always cold. Oh my God, look at these spider burners! I love spider burners.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: So you like it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, it is sooo perfect. Thank you so much. (runs over and hugs him)

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Oh, you're welcome. (He takes a deep breath)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you just smell my hair?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Nooo. Uh-huh, no way. What? No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You still have feelings for me don't you?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Now, nooo! I'm just excited about the restaurant, that's all.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pete.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Okay, I love you. Is that so bad?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it's not bad. It's not bad at all. It's-it's really nice.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Look, the only who stands to get hurt is me. And I'm okay with that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You may be okay about getting hurt, but I am not okay with being the one who hurts you. That's why I can't take this job.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And well, we probably shouldn't see each other anymore. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Okay, yeah. I mean... If that's, if that's really what you want, okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, bye.
She kisses him on the cheek, and he kisses her back on the mouth.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: I'm sorry things didn't work out...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right shut up for a second and let me just see something. (She kisses him back on the lips) Oh, wow! (They then hug and kiss, very passionately.)
The Hallway Between The Apartments, Ross and Rachel are coming back from the hospital. Ross is helping her up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you'd tell me the truth. Right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, you can't look fat in an x-ray.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
As they approach the door, Chandler comes out carrying his duck.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Now you stay out here, and you think about what you did!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Chandler) That's a duck.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's a bad duck!!! (to Ross) How'd the thing go tonight, Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, it was, nah, well....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What thing? What thing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing, ah there was this thing at the museum. Come on. (they go into her apartment) Easy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to the duck) Okay, now when you come back I hope you remember that, that chick is not a toy! (He goes back into the apartment)
[cut to inside Monica and Rachel's]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What thing? What is this thing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was kinda, supposed to be on TV tonight for The Discovery Channel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, why didn't you tell me that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Eh, 'cause I knew that if I told you, you'd make me go, and I knew you needed someone to be with you tonight. Come on. Come on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I cannot believe you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is the sweetest thing, I just....
They both look at each other for a while

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (breaking the silence) You should get some sleep.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, I'll umm...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry I spoiled you're evening.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, that's, no, as long as you're okay. So I'll ah, I'll see you tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um-hmm, yeah.
He leaves

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (After he closes the door) See ya.
In the hallway, Ross all dejected, sits down on the step.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (coming out of his apartment and seeing Ross) What did you do?
Closing Credits
Chandler and Joey's Bathroom: Chandler is watching the duck swim in the bathtub.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What 'cha doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Having a swim.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What about the chick?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Chicks don't swim.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know. Should we try it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure.
Chandler picks up the chick and drops it in the water.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, I told you they don't swim. (He goes to take it out)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stopping him) Wait. Give him a minute.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Noo! (takes him out) Oh, it's okay, it's okay, baby, baby, baby.
Joey picks up and turns on a hair dryer.
End
